goal: Create git-server Go REST API in cluster/agent/gitserver/, following the exact patterns of the fileserver (cluster/agent/fileserver/) and tester (cluster/tester/) for code structure, unit tests, vuln scans, and documentation.

tasks.name: Create gitserver main.go
tasks.action:
Create the Go REST server at cluster/agent/gitserver/main.go. Follow the fileserver pattern exactly: closure-based handlers, setupRouter() for testability, verifyToken() with constant-time comparison, TLS 1.3 minimum, port 8443. Token env var: GIT_API_TOKEN. Cert paths: /app/certs/git.crt and /app/certs/git.key.

Endpoints (from PLAN_GIT_MCP.md):
- GET /status?submodule_path=... → runs git status --short
- GET /diff?staged=bool&submodule_path=... → runs git diff [--cached]
- POST /add (JSON: {paths:[...]}) → runs git add
- POST /commit (JSON: {message, submodule_path}) → runs git commit
- GET /log?max_count=N&submodule_path=... → runs git log --oneline
- POST /reset (JSON: {count, submodule_path}) → runs git reset --soft HEAD~N
- GET /health → 200 OK (no auth)

Port ALL logic from git_mcp.py:
- _run_git helper: exec git with GIT_DIR/GIT_WORK_TREE env, always add -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null
- parse_gitmodules(): parse .gitmodules for submodule name/path
- git_env_for(): resolve correct GIT_DIR+GIT_WORK_TREE for root repo or submodule
- Baseline commit enforcement: read GIT_BASELINE_COMMIT env at startup; git_reset rejects resets past baseline using git merge-base --is-ancestor
- git_add multi-repo guard: all paths must resolve to same repo
- git_commit: always --no-verify, always -c core.hooksPath=/dev/null
- git_log: clamp max_count to 1-50, default 10

Use only Go stdlib (no external deps). Log operations like fileserver: GIT_STATUS, GIT_DIFF, GIT_ADD, GIT_COMMIT, GIT_LOG, GIT_RESET.
tasks.verify: go vet ./... passes; go build -o git-server main.go succeeds
tasks.done: main.go exists with all 7 endpoints, compiles cleanly, follows fileserver handler pattern

tasks.name: Create gitserver unit tests
tasks.action:
Create comprehensive unit tests in cluster/agent/gitserver/main_test.go following the tester's main_test.go pattern (httptest.NewServer + setupRouter).

Test helpers:
- newTestRepo(t): creates temp dir with git init --separate-git-dir, initial commit, returns (gitDir, workTree, cleanup). Set GIT_BASELINE_COMMIT via t.Setenv.
- authRequest(method, url, body, token): builds http.Request with Bearer header

Test cases (mirror git_mcp_test.py coverage):
- TestStatusClean, TestStatusUntracked, TestStatusModified
- TestDiffUnstaged, TestDiffStaged
- TestAddFiles, TestAddAll, TestAddEmptyPaths (400)
- TestCommitBasic, TestCommitEmptyMessage (400), TestCommitNothingToCommit
- TestLogDefault, TestLogMaxCount, TestLogClamp50
- TestResetSoftBasic, TestResetPastBaseline (blocked), TestResetToBaseline (allowed)
- TestHookPrevention: create a malicious hook, run commit, verify hook not executed
- TestSubmoduleRouting: test git_env_for with submodule_path param
- TestAddMultiRepoGuard: paths spanning repos rejected
- TestUnauthorized: all endpoints reject missing/bad token
- TestTLSMinVersion13: TLS 1.2 client rejected, TLS 1.3 succeeds
- TestHealth: returns 200 without auth

Use t.TempDir() for all temp dirs. Use only Go stdlib.
tasks.verify: run_tests passes — specifically: cd cluster/agent/gitserver && GOTOOLCHAIN=local CGO_ENABLED=0 GOMAXPROCS=1 go test -p 1 -cpu 1 -v -timeout 60s ./...
tasks.done: main_test.go exists with 15+ test functions covering all endpoints, security, and edge cases; all tests pass

tasks.name: Create gitserver go.mod, entrypoint.sh, test.sh
tasks.action:
Create three supporting files:

1. cluster/agent/gitserver/go.mod:
```
module gitserver
go 1.26.1
```
No external dependencies (stdlib only, matching fileserver).

2. cluster/agent/gitserver/entrypoint.sh — follow fileserver/tester pattern:
- set -e
- Reject forbidden env vars: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY, CLAUDE_API_TOKEN, DYNAMIC_AGENT_KEY, MCP_API_TOKEN, PLAN_API_TOKEN, TESTER_API_TOKEN
- Require GIT_API_TOKEN present
- Capture baseline: if GIT_DIR and GIT_WORK_TREE set, run git rev-parse HEAD → export GIT_BASELINE_COMMIT
- Parse submodule baselines: for each submodule in .gitmodules, capture HEAD → export GIT_SUBMODULE_BASELINES as JSON
- Verify /workspace mounted
- Verify /gitdir mounted
- exec /app/git-server

3. cluster/agent/gitserver/test.sh — follow tester/test.sh pattern:
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail
echo '[unit] Running Go git-server tests...'
CGO_ENABLED=0 GOMAXPROCS=1 go vet ./...
GOTOOLCHAIN=local CGO_ENABLED=0 GOMAXPROCS=1 go test -p 1 -cpu 1 -v -timeout 60s ./...
```
tasks.verify: run_tests passes — entrypoint.sh is executable (chmod +x); test.sh is executable; go.mod has correct module name and Go version
tasks.done: All three files exist with correct content; entrypoint.sh rejects forbidden tokens and captures baselines; test.sh runs go vet + go test

tasks.name: Add gitserver to agent test.sh
tasks.action:
Add gitserver unit tests to the agent's test.sh, following the existing fileserver pattern.

After the fileserver test block:
```
echo '[unit] Running Go fileserver tests...'
(cd fileserver && GOTOOLCHAIN=local CGO_ENABLED=0 GOMAXPROCS=1 go test -p 1 -cpu 1 mcp_test.go main.go -v)
```

Add:
```
echo '[unit] Running Go git-server tests...'
(cd gitserver && GOTOOLCHAIN=local CGO_ENABLED=0 GOMAXPROCS=1 go test -p 1 -cpu 1 -v -timeout 60s ./...)
```

Also add GIT_API_TOKEN to the dummy token exports at the top:
```
export GIT_API_TOKEN="${GIT_API_TOKEN:-dummy-git-token}"
```
tasks.verify: run_tests passes — cluster/agent/test.sh runs both fileserver and gitserver tests successfully
tasks.done: test.sh includes gitserver unit test execution and dummy GIT_API_TOKEN; both fileserver and gitserver tests pass when test.sh runs

tasks.name: Add govulncheck for gitserver in test-integration.sh
tasks.action:
Add govulncheck scanning for the gitserver in test-integration.sh, following the existing fileserver/tester pattern.

In the govulncheck section (after the tester scan block), add:
```bash
  echo '  Scanning gitserver...'
  GOVULN_GS=$(cd cluster/agent/gitserver && go run golang.org/x/vuln/cmd/govulncheck@latest ./... 2>&1)
  if echo "$GOVULN_GS" | grep -q 'No vulnerabilities found'; then
    echo '  ✅ gitserver govulncheck clean'
  else
    echo "$GOVULN_GS" | tail -5
  fi
```
tasks.verify: grep -q 'gitserver' test-integration.sh confirms the section exists; script syntax is valid (bash -n test-integration.sh passes)
tasks.done: test-integration.sh includes govulncheck scan for gitserver alongside fileserver and tester scans

tasks.name: Create gitserver documentation
tasks.action:
Create documentation following the tester docs pattern exactly.

1. cluster/agent/gitserver/docs/CONTEXT.md:
- Title: 'secure-claude-gitserver: Project Context'
- What This Is: Go REST server that executes git operations against the repository mounted at /workspace with git data at /gitdir. Part of the secure-claude cluster.
- Architecture diagram: claude-server → POST/GET endpoints → git-server:8443 → git subprocess
- Endpoints table: all 7 endpoints (status, diff, add, commit, log, reset, health) with method, path, auth, description
- Response format examples for each endpoint
- Security section: UID 1000, TLS with internal CA, Bearer token (GIT_API_TOKEN), entrypoint.sh rejects forbidden secrets, hook prevention (core.hooksPath=/dev/null + --no-verify + tmpfs shadow), baseline commit floor, workspace read-only
- Token isolation table (matching PLAN_GIT_MCP.md)
- Configuration table: GIT_API_TOKEN, GIT_DIR, GIT_WORK_TREE, GIT_BASELINE_COMMIT, SSL_CERT_FILE
- Decisions table: Language=Go (matches fileserver/tester), Hook prevention=3 layers, Baseline enforcement=server-side

2. cluster/agent/gitserver/docs/PLAN.md:
- Empty plan file with header: '# Git Server Development Plan' and note that initial implementation is complete
tasks.verify: Both files exist and contain the required sections; CONTEXT.md documents all 7 endpoints and security model
tasks.done: docs/CONTEXT.md and docs/PLAN.md exist with complete documentation matching tester docs pattern
